moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and stability, as it's an uptrending quality name.
Reduce SMH and CRWD exposure to manage risk from a downtrending sector and single name concentration.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation by de-risking the portfolio significantly.
A large cash buffer will be held to absorb market shocks.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. However, potential upside is also limited compared to more aggressive strategies.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% and QQQI to 15% to meet concentration limits and address the trim flag.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all positions below stop and reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce SMH to 10% to lower risk from a downtrending sector.
Buy NVDA at 10% as a high-scoring scanner setup, adding quality tech exposure.
This plan balances risk and growth by reducing leverage and adding a top-tier tech name.
A 10% cash buffer will be maintained for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, aiming for growth with a risk-first approach. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing a high-quality tech name. Expect moderate drawdowns, but also a more stable growth trajectory than aggressive modes.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% and QQQI to 10% to meet concentration limits and address the trim flag.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove all positions below stop, clearing underperforming assets.
Buy NVDU at 15% to lean into the highest-scoring scanner setup, maximizing potential upside.
Buy ERX at 10% to add another high R-value scanner setup, diversifying aggressive exposure.
This plan uses leverage and concentration in top setups for stretch growth.
A smaller cash buffer will be maintained, emphasizing active management with strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretch growth by concentrating in high-scoring setups and using leverage. It carries significantly higher risk, including potential for larger drawdowns and the daily decay of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage amplified losses.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10% to reduce broad index exposure and shift towards income.
Increase QQQI to 30% to prioritize steady monthly distributions and income generation.
Sell SMH, CRWD, SOXL, and SPAL to eliminate high-beta, downtrending, and leveraged positions.
This plan focuses on rotating into income-generating ETFs and broad index for stability.
The goal is reliable income over growth, with a focus on low drawdown.
A cash buffer will be maintained to prioritize capital preservation.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces high-beta and leveraged exposure. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff, meaning it's not a guaranteed return or a savings account.
